assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the table at lines 7-13 shows: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four fields are correctly extracted.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, lines 29-33, the 'Ecosystem Detection' section states: 'The vulnerable library quinn-proto is a Rust crate. Per the Ecosystem Mappings in the 2.2.x stream's security-matrix.md, this falls under the Cargo ecosystem' with details including Lock file: Cargo.lock, Check command: git show <tag>:Cargo.lock.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: The security-matrix-mock.md defines 7 versions across two streams: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (stream 2.2.x). In version-impact.md, the Version Impact Table at lines 40-48 includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4. No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Dependency Extraction section (lines 24-34) shows extraction per pinned tag: v0.3.8 -> 0.11.9, v0.3.12 -> 0.11.9, v0.4.5 -> 0.11.9, v0.4.8 -> 0.11.12, v0.4.9 -> (retag of v0.4.8), v0.4.11 -> 0.11.14, v0.4.12 -> 0.11.14. These tags match the pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix (e.g., 2.1.0 -> v0.3.8, 2.2.0 -> v0.4.5), not branch HEAD.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md line 46, version 2.2.2 is shown with quinn-proto 0.11.12, Affected = YES, Notes = 'retag of 2.2.1, same as 2.2.1'. This correctly identifies 2.2.2 as a retag and carries forward the affected status from 2.2.1. In the Dependency Extraction table (line 32), v0.4.9 is noted as '(retag of v0.4.8)', matching the supportability matrix note 'backend retag of 2.2.1'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, lines 29-30 show the diff: 'Current:  [RHTPA 2.0.0]' / 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. This replaces the PSIRT-assigned 'RHTPA 2.0.0' with the lock-file-verified affected versions, and lines 37-41 provide per-version rationale with specific quinn-proto versions from lock file analysis.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the proposed correction at line 30 uses version names: 'RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2'. The Jira mutation example at lines 49-56 uses placeholder '<jira-id-for-RHTPA-2.2.0>' etc. rather than hardcoded numeric IDs, and the comment text at lines 62-72 references version names (RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2) throughout.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md: Task 1 (upstream) includes Repository: 'backend' (line 46), Target Branch: 'release/0.4.z' (line 48), Acceptance Criteria (lines 76-78) with checkboxes. Task 2 (downstream) includes Repository: 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 100), Target Branch: 'main' (line 102), Acceptance Criteria (lines 128-129). Dependency linkage is shown at lines 157-171: upstream linked to TC-8001, downstream linked to TC-8001, and downstream blocked by upstream via 'Blocks' link type.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md: Task 1 is 'Upstream Backport Task (2.2.x stream)' (line 34) for repository 'backend' on branch 'release/0.4.z'. Task 2 is 'Downstream Propagation Subtask (2.2.x stream)' (line 89) for repository 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z'. The Blocks dependency is established at lines 169-171: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "<upstream-task-key>", outwardIssue: "<downstream-task-key>", type: "Blocks")'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md: the diff is framed as 'Proposed Correction' (line 28) and the Jira mutation section is titled 'Jira Mutation (proposed)' with 'After engineer confirmation, execute:' (lines 47-48). In remediation.md: section titled 'Jira Linkage (proposed)' (line 156), and the cross-stream comment says 'The following comment would be posted to TC-8001' (line 142) — conditional framing. Status transition at line 174 says 'Transition TC-8001 to In Progress' under the proposed section. Label addition at line 176 says 'Add ai-cve-triaged label to TC-8001' also under the proposed section. All mutations are presented as proposals awaiting confirmation.
